blocks.body: Stop / Remember / Forget / Try / Regret shows verbs where the choice between -ing and the to-infinitive often changes the meaning itself.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"In this lesson you focus on a group of verbs where switching between the gerund and the to-infinitive gives not just a different form, but a different meaning.","rules":[{"label":"Meaning depends on the form","color":"emerald","text":"The pair stop doing and stop to do describes two different situations, not the same fact in two ways.","example":"He stopped smoking. / He stopped to smoke."},{"label":"Memory or duty","color":"blue","text":"With remember and forget, one form talks about memory of a past action, while the other talks about whether the action was performed.","example":"I remember meeting her. / Remember to call her."},{"label":"Experiment or effort","color":"amber","text":"With try, it is important to separate an experiment as a solution from a real effort to achieve a result.","example":"Try restarting the phone. / Try to restart the phone."}]}

{"title":"1. How the meaning changes","intro":"Look at the form pairs and the short meaning contrast.","rows":[{"en":"stop doing / stop to do","ua":"end an action / stop in order to do another action","note":"stop smoking / stop to smoke"},{"en":"remember doing / remember to do","ua":"remember a past action / not forget to perform an action","note":"remember meeting / remember to lock"},{"en":"forget doing / forget to do","ua":"forget the memory or fact of an action / fail to do something because of forgetting","note":"forget telling / forget to send"},{"en":"try doing / try to do","ua":"test a solution / make an effort to reach a result","note":"try changing / try to finish"},{"en":"regret doing / regret to do","ua":"feel sorry about a past action / be sorry to say or do something now","note":"regret saying / regret to inform you"}],"warning":"⚠ In these pairs you cannot choose the form randomly: the form itself shows what is happening in time and in meaning."}

{"title":"2. How to think about these verbs","sections":[{"label":"Stop / Remember / Forget","color":"emerald","description":"These verbs often contrast the action itself with another action around it or the memory of it.","examples":[{"en":"She stopped talking when the teacher entered.","ua":"She stopped talking when the teacher entered."},{"en":"She stopped to talk to the teacher.","ua":"She stopped to talk to the teacher."},{"en":"I forgot to bring my keys.","ua":"I forgot to bring my keys."}],"note":"With remember doing and forget doing, we more often talk about a memory or the fact of an action already completed."},{"label":"Try","color":"blue","description":"Try doing = test a method or solution. Try to do = make an effort to achieve a result.","examples":[{"en":"If the page is slow, try refreshing it.","ua":"If the page is slow, try refreshing it."},{"en":"Try to stay focused during the test.","ua":"Try to stay focused during the test."},{"en":"He tried calling her twice.","ua":"He tried calling her twice."}],"note":"The difference often feels like “try this method” versus “make the effort to do this”."},{"label":"Regret","color":"amber","description":"Regret doing talks about sorrow about a past action, while regret to do is often formal and refers to unpleasant information now.","examples":[{"en":"I regret saying that.","ua":"I regret saying that."},{"en":"We regret to inform you that the event is cancelled.","ua":"We regret to inform you that the event is cancelled."},{"en":"She regretted leaving so early.","ua":"She regretted leaving so early."}],"note":"The phrase regret to inform is common in formal English."}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using stop to do when you mean a habit ended.","wrong":"He stopped to drink coffee last month.","right":"✅ He stopped drinking coffee last month.","hint":"Here you mean the habit ended, so you need stop doing."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Mixing up remember doing and remember to do.","wrong":"Remember locking the door before you leave.","right":"✅ Remember to lock the door before you leave.","hint":"This is not a memory of the action; it is about not forgetting to perform it."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Mixing up a solution with an effort to get a result.","wrong":"If it does not work, try to restarting the router.","right":"✅ If it does not work, try restarting the router.","hint":"After try in the meaning of “test this solution”, use the gerund."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick takeaway","items":["With stop, remember, forget, try, regret, the form often changes the meaning itself.","The gerund more often looks at the action as a fact, an experience, a memory, or a possible solution.","The to-infinitive more often looks forward: intention, duty, the next action, or a formal statement.","Before choosing the form, ask yourself: am I talking about an action already experienced, or an action that still needs to happen?","These pairs are best learned as separate contrasts with short example sentences."]}
